title: Minimum Number of Operations to Make Array XOR Equal to K
titleCn: 使数组异或和等于 K 的最少操作次数

testcases.input: nums = [2,1,3,4], k = 1 Output: 2 Explanation: We can do the following operations: - Choose element 2 which is 3 == (011)2, we flip the first bit and we obtain (010)2 == 2. nums becomes [2,1,2,4]. - Choose element 0 which is 2 == (010)2, we flip the third bit and we obtain (110)2 = 6. nums becomes [6,1,2,4]. The XOR of elements of the final array is (6 XOR 1 XOR 2 XOR 4) == 1 == k. It can be shown that we cannot make the XOR equal to k in less than 2 operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: We can do the following operations: - Choose element 2 which is 3 == (011)2, we flip the first bit and we obtain (010)2 == 2. nums becomes [2,1,2,4]. - Choose element 0 which is 2 == (010)2, we flip the third bit and we obtain (110)2 = 6. nums becomes [6,1,2,4]. The XOR of elements of the final array is (6 XOR 1 XOR 2 XOR 4) == 1 == k. It can be shown that we cannot make the XOR equal to k in less than 2 operations.

testcases.input: nums = [2,0,2,0], k = 0 Output: 0 Explanation: The XOR of elements of the array is (2 XOR 0 XOR 2 XOR 0) == 0 == k. So no operation is needed.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: The XOR of elements of the array is (2 XOR 0 XOR 2 XOR 0) == 0 == k. So no operation is needed.
